

There are three types of practitioners of persuasion:

The Bungler

The Smuggler

And the Sleuth

 

The Bungler of influence either doesn’t know the Principles of Persuasion, or doesn’t understand how to use them. They fumble away influence opportunities that are right there in front of them, ready to be used, ready to be engaged. 



The Smuggler, on the other hand does know the Principles of Persuasion, but chooses to use them illicitly… dishonestly, so that only the Smuggler wins. The Smuggler will fudge the facts, will fabricate or counterfeit the principles with the goal of winning at any cost. Nobody wants to be known as a Smuggler because, in the long run, dishonest gains become losses. 



The Sleuth of influence knows the Principles, knows how to use them, and knows how to use them ethically - using only honest information so that everyone wins. Just as importantly, the sleuth of influence does not manipulate, but instead, informs others into YES so that they make wise choices. As a Cialdini Certified Professional, you must be known as a Sleuth of Influence.  Everything in this workshop is designed around achieving those goals for you.
